topics.topic: Isolating AI coding agents with dev containers, read-only mounts, explicit network policy, and microVM-backed runtimes so they can run useful workflows without broad host access
topics.title: Sandboxing AI Coding Agents Without Killing Developer Velocity
topics.summary: A practical guide to isolating AI coding agents with narrow writable mounts, read-only supporting files, explicit network controls, and stronger runtime boundaries when containers alone are not enough.

topics.topic: Using AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, repo maps, verification commands, and scoped edit rules to make AI coding agents safer, more consistent, and easier for humans to review
topics.title: Repo Playbooks for AI Coding Agents That Actually Change Behavior
topics.summary: A practical guide to writing repository playbooks for AI coding agents with repo maps, no-go zones, verification commands, and review-friendly output rules that reduce drift and improve pull requests.

topics.topic: Using Cursor with layered rules, repo maps, scoped context, and verification commands to keep AI edits accurate and contained inside large monorepos
topics.title: Cursor Rules for Monorepos That Keep AI Edits on the Rails
topics.summary: A practical guide to using Cursor in large monorepos with layered rules, local context, blast-radius guardrails, and verification loops that keep AI edits narrow and reviewable.

topics.topic: Reviewing AI-generated pull requests with scoped diffs, invariant checklists, CI guardrails, and CODEOWNERS-based risk controls so teams can merge faster without rubber-stamping
topics.title: Reviewing AI-Generated Pull Requests Without Becoming the Bottleneck
topics.summary: A practical guide to reviewing AI-generated pull requests with scoped diffs, invariant checklists, CI guardrails, and risk-based review loops so teams can move quickly without rubber-stamping machine-made code.

topics.topic: Using prompt caching, stable prompt prefixes, context compaction, and retrieval boundaries to reduce latency and cost in tool-heavy AI agents without degrading task quality
topics.title: Prompt Caching for Tool-Heavy AI Agents That Need to Stay Fast
topics.summary: A practical guide to reducing latency and cost in tool-heavy AI agents by freezing the reusable prompt prefix, pushing dynamic context later in the request, compacting long-running state, and measuring cache effectiveness.

topics.topic: Tracing AI agents with OpenTelemetry and OpenInference to debug tool calls, retrieval failures, latency, token usage, and multi-step execution paths
topics.title: Tracing AI Agents with OpenTelemetry Without Drowning in Logs
topics.summary: A practical guide to tracing AI agents with OpenTelemetry and OpenInference so teams can inspect nested model/tool spans, track cost and latency, label failures, and debug workflows without relying on flat logs.

topics.topic: Building a practical local LLM development environment with Ollama, Open WebUI, coding clients, quantization tradeoffs, prompt versioning, and explicit hosted-model fallback rules
topics.title: Local LLM Dev Environments That Stay Fast, Cheap, and Private
topics.summary: A practical guide to setting up local LLMs for daily developer work with Ollama, Open WebUI, coding-focused clients, prompt/version control, smoke tests, and smart escalation to hosted models when local ones are not enough.

topics.topic: Evaluating retrieval-augmented generation systems with separate retrieval and answer metrics, grounded golden sets, failure labeling, and regression checks
topics.title: Practical RAG Evaluation Techniques That Catch Real Failures
topics.summary: A practical guide to evaluating RAG systems by splitting retrieval from generation, building realistic test sets, tracking failure categories, and running regressions that catch breakage before users do.

topics.topic: Designing durable memory for AI agents with working state, episodic summaries, semantic facts, scoped retrieval, and explicit write policies
topics.title: Agent Memory Strategies That Don't Rot in Production
topics.summary: A practical guide to designing agent memory that stays useful in production by separating working memory, episodes, and semantic facts while controlling writes, retrieval, and drift.

topics.topic: Using Claude Code to plan and execute large refactors in phases with explicit checkpoints and verification loops
topics.title: Claude Code Workflows for Large Refactors Without Losing the Plot
topics.summary: A practical guide to using Claude Code for large refactors with short specs, phased execution, move-first edits, review passes, and explicit verification.

topics.topic: Hardening a self-hosted OpenClaw deployment on a VPS without losing useful automations
topics.title: How to Harden OpenClaw on a VPS Without Breaking the Good Parts
topics.summary: A practical guide to securing OpenClaw on a VPS with least-privilege credentials, reduced network exposure, approval boundaries, cron hygiene, and operational visibility.

topics.topic: Practical security patterns for MCP servers used by tool-calling agents
topics.title: Secure MCP Server Design Patterns for Real Tool-Calling Agents
topics.summary: A practical tutorial on designing MCP servers with narrow tools, write approvals, server-side validation, session isolation, prompt-injection defenses, and structured observability.
